name: AI Video Generation
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: Video generation orchestration — script creation from text prompts, scene planning, style transfer guidance, storyboard generation, and quality assessment of generated frames

dev.note: ~80K tokens/day, script generation and frame quality checks

prod.note: ~500K tokens/day, parallel scene planning, quality assessment

enterprise.note: Provisioned throughput for high-volume video pipeline

services.name: Azure Container Apps
services.purpose: Video rendering pipeline — GPU-enabled containers for frame generation, video encoding, post-processing, format conversion, and thumbnail extraction

services.name: Blob Storage
services.purpose: Video asset storage — generated videos, intermediate frames, audio tracks, thumbnails, storyboard images, and source prompt archives

services.name: Azure AI Content Safety
services.purpose: Video safety moderation — frame-by-frame analysis for harmful visual content, text overlay screening, audio transcript safety checks, NSFW detection

services.name: Service Bus
services.purpose: Video pipeline orchestration — job queue for rendering tasks, scene sequencing, encoding stages, safety review gates, and delivery notifications

services.name: Azure CDN
services.purpose: Video delivery — global distribution of generated videos, adaptive bitrate streaming, thumbnail serving, and low-latency playback

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: API keys for AI services, storage SAS tokens, CDN credentials, Content Safety keys, watermarking certificates

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Pipeline telemetry — rendering duration, queue depth, safety rejection rates, GPU utilization, video quality scores, delivery latency

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for initial storyboard generation and scene planning — reserve GPT-4o Vision for final frame quality assessment, reducing orchestration costs by 80%
Implement frame-level caching for common visual elements (backgrounds, transitions, logos) — reuse rendered assets across videos to reduce GPU rendering time by 40%
Use Container Apps scale-to-zero during off-hours — schedule rendering jobs during low-cost periods and batch process overnight for non-urgent video requests
Apply Content Safety checks on keyframes only (every 2 seconds) instead of every frame — interpolated frames between safe keyframes are statistically safe, reducing moderation costs by 90%
Implement tiered storage lifecycle — move completed videos to Cool storage after 7 days and Archive after 30 days, reducing storage costs by 70% for long-tail content
Use adaptive resolution rendering — generate 720p previews for approval, only render final 1080p/4K after client sign-off to avoid wasting GPU on rejected concepts
